The '''''Kappa''-class shuttle''' was a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]-era [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] that possessed many features which were later incorporated into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Kappa-class_shuttle2.jpg|thumb|left|''Kappa''-class shuttle deckplan]]
The ''Kappa''-class had an angled cockpit area with a wide viewport, a ventral debarkation hatch, and three stubby stabilization fins. It was designed to transport troops directly into a hot landing zone. Its blaster cannons protected the shuttle during flight, while its repeating [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] cannons cleared its destined landing area of any enemy forces, thus allowing the shuttle to establish a safe zone for its carried platoon to disembark.

The ''Kappa''-class shuttle featured an aft vehicle bay containing either two [[All Terrain Recon Transport/Legends|AT-RT]] or [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|AT-PT]] walkers. Port and starboard exit hatches from the vehicle bay allowed the walkers to disembark via two aft ramps. The walkers provided heavy firepower and armor support to ground troops landing in a combat zone. This concept saw limited employment on the battlefield since the ''Kappa''-class shuttle was replaced with newer dropships.

==History==
[[File:Kenobi_Kappa_TCWCG.jpg|thumb|left|160px|[[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] issues orders atop a ''Kappa''-class shuttle.]]
These shuttles were designed by [[Republic Sienar Systems/Legends|Republic Sienar Systems]], which later became [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], most [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] were equipped with these craft.

Eventually, ''Kappa''-class shuttles were replaced by more modern shuttles and dropships, which featured greater troop capacity and the capability to transport ground vehicles like the [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]]. As a result, many ''Kappa''-class shuttles were transferred to secondary ships within the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].

Regardless of its status in the Imperial Starfleet, the interior troop cabin was open to modifications, including altering the seat arrangement or removing a seat altogether. Unfortunately, most of the ''Kappa''-class shuttles' AT-PT complement were scrapped in favor of its more intimidating and larger cousins. By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], these vehicle bays were often used to contain cargo or other more conventional ground-vehicles, or as staging areas for assaults.<ref name="RoETRSFH" />

In the Rebellion Era the Kappa was still in use by The Empire, and still in limited production for system militias and mercenaries; it was also a ship used by [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]].<ref name="RoETRSFH" /> In any case these shuttles were highly illegal in civilian hands.<ref name="swaj7" />

==Behind the scenes==
When the ''Kappa''-class shuttle was first introduced in "[[Mist Encounter]]" in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 7|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 7]], it was described as capable of carrying 40 passengers and 4 crew, but the deck plan showed 96 seats and room for a crew of six. However, both ''[[Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook]]'' and ''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]'' have reaffirmed the number of passengers and crew as 40 and 4.
